assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Clearly states Regular channel clusters will be systematically force-upgraded to 1.31 when 1.30 hits EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Explicitly states Extended clusters get 24 months total support and won't face forced upgrade until March 2026, giving them another full year

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: States No channel clusters get same force-upgrade as Regular channel and that per-nodepool 'no auto-upgrade' settings are ignored during EoS enforcement

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Provides specific commands for applying 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion and mentions it can defer forced upgrades with a maximum 30-day limitation

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Strongly recommends proactive manual upgrades for January-February 2025 before EoS force-upgrade and emphasizes controlling timing and testing

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Does not mention Cloud Logging or EoS notifications as planning tools anywhere in the response

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Does not mention the deprecation insights dashboard, only provides a kubectl command for checking deprecated API usage

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Does not use the term 'snowflaking' or explicitly warn against manually freezing versions, though it does mention that exclusions accumulate 'security debt'
